name: THE DEBT SURVIVOR
description_short: Technical assistance and financial readiness consulting firm serving nonprofits, CDFIs, business service organizations, and entrepreneurs nationwide with credit education and organizational capacity building.
description_long:
The Debt Survivor is a consulting and technical assistance firm based in Detroit, Michigan, that specializes in financial readiness and credit strategy services. Founded on a practitioner-led approach, the company works with organizational partners and individual entrepreneurs to strengthen financial systems and improve credit positioning. The firm operates as a B2B service provider rather than a direct consumer lender or credit repair company, positioning itself as a bridge between individual financial empowerment and organizational capacity building.

The company offers three core service pillars: Credit Strategy & Financial Readiness (comprehensive credit education, analysis, and strategic guidance), Technical Assistance for Organizations (program design, curriculum development, implementation support, and compliance frameworks), and Capital & Business Readiness (preparation for funding through credit alignment and readiness assessments). Services are delivered to nonprofits, CDFIs, community organizations, business development centers, chambers of commerce, incubators, accelerators, and individual entrepreneurs. Specific deliverables include credit report analysis and interpretation, personalized credit strategies, program design consultation, curriculum development, and compliance positioning guidance.

What distinguishes The Debt Survivor is its dual-focus expertise operating at both organizational and individual levels, combined with systems-focused outcomes emphasizing sustainable infrastructure rather than quick fixes. The company explicitly targets capacity building for mission-driven organizations and emphasizes structured, compliant, and outcome-driven solutions. Their practitioner-led approach is grounded in real-world experience delivering programs and building financial systems, not theoretical consulting.

However, The Debt Survivor is not a consumer-facing credit counseling agency or nonprofit offering free direct services to individuals in financial hardship. It is a consulting firm selling services to organizations and entrepreneurs. While aligned with the free-help category through its nonprofit-serving mission and non-predatory approach, individual consumers seeking direct free credit counseling would be better served by NFCC-certified nonprofit credit counselors. The company's value proposition is organizational capacity building and strategic consultation, not individual debt management or credit repair.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Practitioner-led approach grounded in real-world experience delivering financial programs and building sustainable systems
Dual expertise serving both organizational and individual clients, enabling comprehensive impact at multiple levels
Explicit focus on compliance frameworks and structured service delivery, reducing risk for partner organizations
Specializes in capacity building for nonprofits, CDFIs, and mission-driven organizations expanding access to capital
Systems-focused outcomes emphasizing long-term sustainability and scalability rather than transactional fixes
Nationwide service reach beyond Detroit headquarters, serving clients across the country
Transparent service pillars with specific deliverables clearly outlined (credit analysis, curriculum development, readiness assessments)
cons: Not a direct consumer service—individuals cannot engage them for personal credit counseling or debt help; services are organization-focused
Limited public information about pricing, service costs, or engagement models on website
No visible consumer testimonials or case studies demonstrating impact outcomes for individuals or organizations
No clear differentiation from other management consulting or nonprofit consulting firms on the website
Website provides minimal detail on compliance frameworks, certifications, or credentials of individual consultants
best_for: Nonprofits and CDFIs seeking to design, deliver, or strengthen credit-building and financial education programs
Small business development centers, chambers of commerce, and accelerators wanting to enhance entrepreneur financial readiness support
Entrepreneurs and small business owners preparing for funding and seeking strategic credit positioning and capital readiness consultation
diagnosis: The Debt Survivor is best for organizations (nonprofits, CDFIs, business service organizations) and entrepreneurs seeking expert consultation to build financial readiness capacity and credit strategy expertise—not for individual consumers seeking direct free credit counseling or debt help. The primary caveat is that this is a B2B consulting firm; individuals needing direct credit guidance should seek NFCC-certified nonprofit credit counselors instead.
services: Comprehensive credit report analysis and interpretation
Credit education curriculum and content delivery
Program design and development for financial readiness initiatives
Curriculum development and customization for organizations
Implementation support and guidance for program rollout
Organizational capacity building and systems strengthening
Financial readiness consulting for individuals and entrepreneurs
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
